claim_boundary: formal obligation ledger records typed applicability, invariant, estimator-boundary, and reviewer obligations; it is not a PQC break claim

families.claim_boundary: formal obligations are applicability checks, not PQC break claims

family_invariants.statement: n > 0 and q > 1
secret and error distributions are present for LWE/MLWE

proof_obligations.statement: Target parameters satisfy n > 0, q > 1, and m/k bounds where present.
Secret and error distributions are specified for LWE/MLWE.
primal_usvp beta is positive and bounded by the target dimension policy.
Evaluator outputs are analytical or toy plumbing results, not security-break proofs.

secret and error distributions are present for LWE/MLWE

families.claim_boundary: formal obligations are applicability checks, not PQC break claims

family_invariants.statement: NTRU schema-only targets carry positive n/q parameters and a secret distribution

proof_obligations.statement: Schema-only NTRU plans must not emit cryptanalytic estimates.
Evaluator outputs are analytical or toy plumbing results, not security-break proofs.

families.claim_boundary: formal obligations are applicability checks, not PQC break claims

families.claim_boundary: formal obligations are applicability checks, not PQC break claims

family_invariants.statement: n > 0, k > 0, w > 0, and k <= n

families.claim_boundary: formal obligations are applicability checks, not PQC break claims

family_invariants.statement: variables > 0, equations > 0, and field is present

proof_obligations.statement: Multivariate AttackPlans require positive variables, positive equations, and a declared finite field.
Evaluator outputs are analytical or toy plumbing results, not security-break proofs.

families.claim_boundary: formal obligations are applicability checks, not PQC break claims

family_invariants.statement: hash_function is present and n > 0 when provided

proof_obligations.statement: Hash-based bound checks are applicability/routing checks, not attack-success claims.
Evaluator outputs are analytical or toy plumbing results, not security-break proofs.

families.claim_boundary: formal obligations are applicability checks, not PQC break claims

family_invariants.statement: n > 0 and the family is historical-scope only

proof_obligations.statement: Historical isogeny tasks are toy/historical routing checks and cannot describe current-standard break claims.
Evaluator outputs are analytical or toy plumbing results, not security-break proofs.

families.claim_boundary: formal obligations are applicability checks, not PQC break claims

proof_obligations.statement: Implementation-security toy checks do not establish conformance, side-channel resistance, or security claims.
